prose:
Frontier-orbital energies for ethylene (C₂H₄) computed at DFT, tier 'standard' → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF (RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet). Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, single-point (not re-optimized at DFT level; labeled B3LYP/def2-TZVP // input xyz). SCF converged in 6 cycles. No warnings reported — clean run. Integrity: status ok, trustworthy true.

Results:
  HOMO (index 7):          −7.6155818 eV
  LUMO (index 8):          −0.17986527 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap:            7.4357165 eV  ← headline value
  Total energy:            −78.623486 Hartree (−2139.4541 eV)

Full frontier-orbital ladder:
  HOMO−2 (idx 5): −11.487503 eV
  HOMO−1 (idx 6):  −9.883854 eV
  HOMO   (idx 7):  −7.6155818 eV
  LUMO   (idx 8):  −0.17986527 eV
  LUMO+1 (idx 9):  +1.720103 eV
  LUMO+2 (idx 10): +2.3802006 eV

Koopmans' descriptors: vertical IP = 7.6155818 eV, vertical EA = 0.17986527 eV, chemical hardness η = 3.7178583 eV, electrophilicity index ω = 2.0431452 eV.

Caveats: (1) Orbital energies are Koopmans'-style one-electron eigenvalues, not true excitation energies. (2) Single-point on input geometry — no DFT-level relaxation. (3) No density fitting (exact four-center integrals). (4) Gas phase only.
